name: kitp-web
description: kitp web client — zero-framework, signal/control, esbuild-only.

comments.toolchain: esbuild is the SOLE build/dev dependency. No Svelte, Vite, Tailwind, React, vitest, or jest. Type-checking is via `tsc --noEmit` (typescript provided ambiently / by editor) as the type oracle; esbuild does emit only.
comments.runtime-deps: The Markdown editor + renderer is built on a VENDORED ProseMirror bundle (web/vendor/prosemirror.js — a single self-contained ESM file bundling the prosemirror-* graph + markdown-it; see its banner). It is NOT an npm dependency: built once in a scratch dir via esbuild and committed, like the other vendor/ files. The editor lives behind src/editor/ (RichEditor boundary: markdown string in/out). The render path (src/editor/render.ts, DOMSerializer + createElement, html:false) is the single Markdown display sink via src/util/markdown-control.ts — this replaced the former marked + dompurify pipeline.
comments.tests: node --test runs test/*.test.mjs (esbuild-built on the fly). Dependency-light: no test framework. jsdom is a TEST-ONLY devDependency: it provides a real DOM (DOMParser/createElement/etc.) that Node has no built-in for, so tests that exercise DOM-building code (e.g. the editor render path) install a jsdom window before importing the module under test. jsdom is never bundled into dist and is not part of the build/dev-server path.
